why_it_matters: Infection-prevention counseling for a caregiver of young children is reasonable preventive guidance but has low individual clinical impact if omitted.

Refugee status, non-English language, insurance, and housing are core SDOH that shape communication needs, resources, and care planning at a wellness visit, and the employment detail also conflicts with the caregiver narrative.
A documented 0/10 pain score corroborates the asymptomatic osteoarthritis assessment but adds little beyond what is already described.
A standardized stress finding supports the caregiver-burden and anxiety assessment, though marked resolved it has limited impact on current management.
